danger_signals.visual_form: traps and obstacles placed throughout level terrain
danger_signals.meaning: indicates paths that will harm or kill lemmings if traversed without skill intervention
communicates urgency and constraint—lemmings must reach exit before time expires

danger_signals.visual_form: lemmings failing to reach exit or falling into hazards
danger_signals.meaning: visual feedback that current strategy is failing; world state degradation

safety_signals.visual_form: designated exit point(s) visible on level map
safety_signals.meaning: goal indicator—lemmings reaching this location survive and contribute to success

safety_signals.visual_form: UI display of limited skill pool per level
safety_signals.meaning: communicates available tools to overcome hazards and create safe passages

safety_signals.visual_form: lemmings reaching exit and being counted toward completion
safety_signals.meaning: positive world state change—incremental progress toward level goal

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: thematic world design specific to Highland tribe aesthetic
cave/underground-themed environmental design for Cavelem world
circus-themed visual environment and level design

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: preserves original lemming appearance and behavioral quirks from Lemmings 1
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: visual design echoing original Lemmings aesthetic

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: twelve distinct thematic worlds with visual variations in appearance and cultural design elements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: each tribe's lemmings exhibit cultural exuberance and varied appearances despite identical gameplay mechanics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: twelve worlds with ten levels each; completion marked by obtaining gold/silver/bronze medals
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player must replay earlier levels if insufficient lemmings survive to complete later levels; carries over remaining lemmings between levels

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: golden talisman pieces collected from each world; twelve pieces required to complete game
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: talisman pieces obtained only by achieving gold medal ranking on all ten levels of a world; represents progress toward escaping island doom

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: ancient prophecy of 'great darkness' covering island establishes thematic urgency for all level hazards
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: all levels are framed as escape attempts under time pressure with increasing complexity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: limited pool of skills displayed per level; varied skill availability across different tribes' worlds
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player must strategically allocate scarce resources to guide lemmings safely; some skills more frequent in certain worlds

environmental_storytelling.example: Cavelem world uses underground/cave aesthetic to communicate tribe identity and environmental context
each of twelve tribes inhabits thematically-distinct worlds with culturally-specific visual design, telling implicit stories of tribal identity
prophecy of darkness and island doom frames puzzle-solving as survival mechanism; talisman collection represents gathering power to escape catastrophe
certain skills more frequently available in specific tribal worlds implies each tribe has developed specialized survival techniques

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: autonomous movement toward exit; vulnerable to hazards and obstacles
behavior altered based on skill type—mobility enhancement (Runner, Icarus Wings), terrain modification (Builder, Miner), behavior control (Blocker, Attractor)
death or injury if skill protection not applied; triggers replay/reset incentive
player-controlled propulsion affecting lemmings with relevant skills (Ballooner, Surfer) and powering level mechanisms
preserve original Blocker skill and behavioral quirks from first game; visual/mechanical callback to predecessor

notes: Lemmings 2 communicates world-state primarily through narrative framing (prophecy, talisman quest), tribe-specific environmental design, and skill-constraint mechanics. Danger is signaled through hazards and time limits; safety through exits and skill availability. Factions are distinguished by thematic world design rather than color/symbol systems. The game's manifestation layer emphasizes choice (twelve worlds can be tackled in any order) and consequence (lemming survival carries between levels, requiring strategic replay).
